actionUnits.condition: Neighbor-driven ignition propagation. MonoBehaviour_0 on this GameObject maps exactly to Fire.cs (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d, fileID=11500000). Serialized config: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60 (Awake sets timeToOn ∈ [40,60]), onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2, BoxCollider enabled. Preconditions to ignite: at least two nearby Fire neighbors (within 4 units) report NotifyNeighborState(true) so onNeighbors≥2; one neighbor positioned >=3 units below to set hasDownNeighbor=true. Expected behavior: shouldTurnOn becomes true, timeToOnDelta accumulates (faster if hasDownNeighbor due to downMultiplier). When timeToOnDelta ≥ timeToOn, isOn toggles true, UpdateFireState(true) is called internally: mainEffectGo's ParticleSystem.Play(), neighbors notified, FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1). Note: No parameterless public methods are exposed for manual calls; this relies on neighbor events and Update loop.

Water suppression turn-off path. Same MonoBehaviour (Fire.cs) as above. Preconditions: fire is currently on (isOn=true) from previous test or via setup. Repeated water impacts must invoke Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) such that currentHitInterval remains < maxHitInterval=2 for the entire duration, and cumulative currentHitTime ≥ totalHitTime=5. Expected behavior: On first hit, isTurningOff=true and currentHitInterval resets; with subsequent hits before 2s gap, currentHitTime accumulates; once currentHitTime≥5, fire turns off: isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timers reset, UpdateFireState(true) runs internally causing ParticleSystem.Stop(), neighbors notified, FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: WaterHit(Vector3) is a public method but requires a parameter; there are no public parameterless methods to invoke via Trigger. This test relies on actual collision/interaction from a water projectile/system to call WaterHit automatically.
